canonical_definition: The latent tendency of a model to assign disproportionately positive or negative sentiment to outputs based on socio-demographic attributes of the subject, independent of the actual data.
papers: Agent-to-Agent Theory of Mind: Testing Interlocutor Awareness among Large Language Models
Crisp: Cognitive Restructuring of Negative Thoughts through Multi-turn Supportive Dialogues
